claim: Mice that lack Interferon-γ or its receptor exhibit high resistance to experimental autoimmune myocarditis.

result.claim: Mice that lack Interferon-γ or its receptor exhibit high resistance to experimental autoimmune myocarditis.
result.reasoning: The claim that mice lacking Interferon-γ or its receptor exhibit high resistance to experimental autoimmune myocarditis is refuted by the evidence. Paper ID 24338780 states that "IFN-gamma receptor-deficient mice (IFN-gammaR(-/-)) on a BALB/c background immunized with a peptide derived from cardiac alpha-myosin heavy chain develop severe myocarditis." The abstract of this paper further concludes that "IFN-gamma protects mice from lethal autoimmune myocarditis." Similarly, paper ID 40632104 reports that "IFN-gamma-deficient mice developed fatal autoimmune disease," and its abstract indicates that "IFN-gamma production from these cells limits disease progression." Both sources consistently demonstrate that the absence of IFN-γ or its receptor leads to increased susceptibility and severity of autoimmune myocarditis, rather than resistance.

evidence.text: IL-12p40-deficient mice on a susceptible background resisted myocarditis.
evidence.source: Dual role of the IL-12/IFN-gamma axis in the development of autoimmune myocarditis: induction by IL-12 and protection by IFN-gamma.

evidence.text: IgG2a and IgG2b production were increased in the gp130F759/F759 mice.
evidence.source: Dissection of signaling cascades through gp130 in vivo: reciprocal roles for STAT3- and SHP2-mediated signals in immune responses.

evidence.text: The gp130F759/F759 mice displayed splenomegaly and lymphadenopathy.

evidence.text: Autoimmune disease susceptibility and resistance alleles on mouse chromosome 3 (Idd3) correlate with differential expression of the key immunoregulatory cytokine interleukin-2 (IL-2).
evidence.source: Interleukin-2 gene variation impairs regulatory T cell function and causes autoimmunity

evidence.text: Treatment of wild-type mice with N:-nitro-l-arginine-methyl-ester prevented healing of myocarditis.
evidence.source: Lethal autoimmune myocarditis in interferon-gamma receptor-deficient mice: enhanced disease severity by impaired inducible nitric oxide synthase induction.

evidence.text: IFN-gamma receptor-deficient mice (IFN-gammaR(-/-)) on a BALB/c background immunized with a peptide derived from cardiac alpha-myosin heavy chain develop severe myocarditis.
evidence.source: Lethal autoimmune myocarditis in interferon-gamma receptor-deficient mice: enhanced disease severity by impaired inducible nitric oxide synthase induction.

evidence.text: Interferon-gamma (IFN-gamma) is a cytokine involved in the regulation of inflammatory responses in autoimmune diseases.
